description:
About the job

Hi there! We are South and our client is looking for a Google Ads / PPC Specialist!

Note To Applicants


Eligibility: This position is open to candidates residing in Latin America. 
Application Language: Please submit your CV in English. Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered. 
Professional Presentation: We encourage you to showcase your professional experience by including a Loom video in the application form. While this is optional, candidates who provide a video presentation will be given priority. 
Interview Policy: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools during interviews are strictly prohibited. 
Note: As part of the final stages of our hiring process, candidates may be asked to provide professional references for a reference check. If hired, you may also be asked to update your LinkedIn profile :)


The Role

We’re hiring a hands-on Google Ads / PPC Specialist focused on lead generation through paid search. The goal is simple: turn high-intent searches into phone calls and booked appointments, not clicks or impressions.

This is not e-commerce or ROAS optimization on product catalogs. Success here means driving lower cost per lead, higher lead volume, and stronger lead quality across home services and local service industries such as HVAC, plumbing, roofing, electrical, and healthcare.

You’ll own your accounts end to end — from audits and campaign builds to optimization, scaling, and performance storytelling. You’ll collaborate closely with a copywriter (ads), a web/UX team (landing pages), and account managers (client context and communication).

What You’ll Own


Audit, build, manage, and scale Google Ads campaigns (Search, Local Services Ads / Google Guaranteed, Performance Max, 
Display, and remarketing) across multiple accounts
Improve performance by reducing cost per lead while increasing lead volume and quality
Manage keyword strategy, including match types, search term analysis, negative keywords, and continuous optimization to eliminate wasted spend
Own conversion tracking integrity using GA4, Google Tag Manager, and call tracking tools (e.g., CallRail or similar), ensuring accuracy before optimization
Close the loop with CRM data by feeding qualified leads and booked jobs back into Google Ads via offline conversion imports
Manage bidding strategies and budget allocation to maximize lead efficiency
Align landing pages with ad intent to improve conversion rates (in collaboration with web/UX teams)
Continuously test ad copy, landing pages, bidding strategies, and audiences
Report performance clearly to stakeholders and proactively flag risks or performance shifts
Use AI tools daily to accelerate analysis, reporting, and optimization workflows


The Hard Parts (Read Before Applying)


We prefer candidates self-selecting out if the environment is not a fit:
Lead generation is different from e-commerce. This role focuses on lead quality, not purchase ROAS. Interpreting lead quality requires deeper judgment than transaction-based metrics
Full ownership role. You will both execute campaigns and own outcomes — there is no separate team running accounts for you
Multiple accounts, fast pace. You’ll manage several clients simultaneously, each with different seasonality patterns and performance dynamics
Client communication under pressure. You’ll handle questions when lead volume fluctuates or costs rise, and must be able to explain performance clearly and calmly
Tracking accuracy is critical. Incorrect tracking invalidates all optimization work — validation is a core responsibility


How You’ll Be Measured


Cost per lead (CPL) — reduction and stability across accounts
Lead volume and quality — increased number of qualified leads (calls, booked appointments, form fills)
Client retention — accounts remain due to consistent performance
Account health — clean structure, accurate tracking, proper negatives, and well-documented changes


Who You Are

This role is a strong fit if you:


Have 3+ years of experience managing Google Ads / paid search with measurable results
Can independently audit accounts and prioritize fixes with clarity
Have scaled budgets while maintaining cost-per-lead targets
Have deep expertise in Search campaigns (match types, search term mining, negatives, bidding strategies)
Have experience with Local Services Ads (Google Guaranteed), including setup, optimization, and lead dispute handling
Have implemented and managed conversion tracking (GA4, GTM, call tracking) independently
Have experience feeding offline CRM conversions back into Google Ads (closed-loop attribution)
Are highly data-driven and continuously testing new approaches
Communicate performance clearly and confidently in client-facing settings
Use AI tools (e.g., Claude, ChatGPT or similar) as part of your daily workflow
Are fluent in professional English and comfortable working with U.S.-based teams and clients


Schedule: Monday to Friday, 9 AM – 5 PM U.S. Central Time.

Compensation: USD salary

Location: 100% remote opportunity

If this opportunity sounds good to you, send us your resume!

description:
About the job

About Klar

Let’s start with the basics! Klar is a Mexican fintech whose mission is to democratize and revolutionize how financial services are delivered in Mexico — especially since half of the population still doesn’t have a bank account. Since our launch in 2019, we’ve served more than 7 million users with a growing suite of debit and credit products, and the future is brighter than ever.

Today, we’re not only scaling our consumer business, but also building Klar Empresas (B2B) from the ground up — a new division focused on empowering Mexican SMEs with the financial tools, capital, and services they need to grow. This is an exciting new chapter where we get to design and build from scratch while leveraging the trust and scale of Klar’s platform.

We are also proud to have been certified as a “Great Place to Work” in Mexico — something we’ve worked hard to achieve and will continue to build on.

Our people

If you join us at Klar, you’ll be welcomed to a team which is rich in many talents and we are very proud! With our head office in Mexico City, and remote tech hubs in Berlin and Argentina, we are always learning something new about another culture or language. With so many people from different backgrounds and walks of life (young professionals, parents, LGBTQ+, neurodivergence), you’ll definitely find your people here!

Our values


Ownership - We own our successes & our failures as a team.
Excellence - We do everything to the best of our ability & always seek to achieve a new level of excellence in our work.
Inclusion - We believe we are stronger together and actively work to promote a safe, diverse, inclusive, and respectful culture.
Customer Obsession - We understand the value Klar can bring to its customers & it’s always at the forefront of our decisions.
Klarity - We communicate clearly & with authenticity. It’s in our name & it’s what we do.


The position and your daily adventures

We’re looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist to execute, optimize, and scale paid acquisition campaigns across Google, Meta, and other performance platforms. This is a hands-on role for someone who loves data, experimentation, and turning budgets into measurable growth.

You will own the day-to-day operation of performance campaigns, continuously improving CAC, ROAS, and funnel conversion, working closely with creative, analytics, and growth teams. You’ll be part of a high-performance team managing a US$2M+ monthly budget.

What you can expect:


Reporting: Create weekly and ad-hoc performance reports. Make financial analysis of campaign spend.
Own campaign tracking, attribution setup, and pixel/event health.
Operate and optimize paid campaigns: Constant monitoring and daily adjustments of paid ads campaigns on Google, Facebook and new channels.
Launch new campaigns, ad sets, creatives, and targeting strategies
Monitor performance daily and implement data-driven optimizations.
Run A/B tests on creatives, audiences, landing pages, and funnels.
Collaborate with creative and product teams to improve conversion.
KEY KPIs: New users and approved credit lines, Cost per Acquisition (CpA), and ROAS. 


What we are looking for:


Bachelor's degree in Industrial Engineering, Business Engineering, Digital Transformation Engineering, (or similar) with an orientation to data analysis.
2+ years hands-on experience running performance campaigns on: Meta and Google.
Strong analytical skills, comfort working with performance dashboards and experience with SQL.
Experience with conversion tracking, pixels, and attribution tools.
Ability to do Data-driven decision-making.
High ownership, attention to detail, and execution speed.
Advanced English level.
Experience in fintech, apps, or subscription products is a plus.


Our offer to you:


Competitive salary based on performance and experience
Chance of earning Klar stock options
15 days of paid vacation per year; plus extended maternity and paternity leaves
Vacation premium
Food vouchers
30 days of Christmas bonus
Medical Insurance
Computer devices
Well hub subscription to offer mental and physical health 
Sponsored coaching and therapy sessions 
A modern centrally located office in Mexico City with free drinks, snacks, and regular social events
International work environment with amazing and highly skilled people
A world-class team that helps you evolve your skills in areas you're interested in


Klar is a safe place for everyone!

We trust our highly skilled and diverse team, and we’re committed to creating a welcoming and inclusive environment for new talents to flourish. We value diversity and welcome all applications regardless of gender, nationality, ethnic and social origin, religion/belief, physical abilities, age, sexual orientation and identity.

Should you require any accommodations through the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to let us know how we can help!

description:
About the job

At Medtronic you can begin a life-long career of exploration and innovation, while helping champion healthcare access and equity for all. You’ll lead with purpose, breaking down barriers to innovation in a more connected, compassionate world.

A Day in the Life

Responsibilities may include the following and other duties may be assigned.


Formulates and reviews corporate short and long-range plans, goals, and objectives for specific product or product line.
Conducts competitive product analysis and market research of product consumer behavior and professional audience.
Determines the profitability and viability of new products or product enhancements.
Develops production and marketing strategies and tactics to ensure effective product placement relative to market opportunity, working closely with product development for a specific product, brand, or product area over its entire life cycle.
Works with other functions to finalize product specifications.
Provides advertising with core brand equity.
Serves as technical adviser, demonstrating products and troubleshooting product issues.



SPECIALIST CAREER STREAM: Typically an individual contributor with responsibility in a professional discipline or specialty. Delivers and/or manages projects assigned and works with other stakeholders to achieve desired results. May act as a mentor to colleagues or may direct the work of other lower level professionals. The majority of time is spent delivering and overseeing the projects – from design to implementation - while and adhering to policies, using specialized knowledge and skills normally acquired through advanced education.

DIFFERENTIATING FACTORS

Autonomy: Seasoned individual contributor. Works independently under limited supervision to determine and develop approach to solutions. Coaches and reviews the work of lower level specialists; may manage projects / processes.

Organizational Impact: May be responsible for entire projects or processes within job area. Contributes to the completion of work group objectives, through building relationships and consensus to reach agreements on assignments.

Innovation and Complexity: Problems and issues faced are difficult, and may require understanding of multiple issues, job areas or specialties . Makes improvements of processes, systems or products to enhance performance of the job area. Analysis provided is in-depth in nature and often provides recommendations on process improvements.

Communication and Influence: Communicates with senior internal and external customers and vendors. Exchange information of facts, statuses, ideas and issues to achieve objective, and influence decision-making.

Leadership and Talent Management: May provide guidance, coaching and training to other employees within job area. May manage projects, requiring delegation of work and review of others' work product .

Required Knowledge and Experience: Requires advanced knowledge of job area combining breadth and depth, typically obtained through advanced education combined with experience. May have practical knowledge of project management. Requires a Baccalaureate degree (or for degrees earned outside of the United States, a degree which satisfies the requirements of 8 C.F.R.


214.2(h)( 4)(iii)(A) and minimum of 4 years of relevant experience, or advanced degree with a minimum of 2 years relevant experience.



Physical Job Requirements

The above statements are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by employees assigned to this position, but they are not an exhaustive list of all the required responsibilities and skills of this position. 

Benefits & Compensation

Medtronic offers a competitive Salary and flexible Benefits Package

A commitment to our employees lives at the core of our values. We recognize their contributions. They share in the success they help to create. We offer a wide range of benefits, resources, and competitive compensation plans designed to support you at every career and life stage.

About Medtronic

We lead global healthcare technology and boldly attack the most challenging health problems facing humanity by searching out and finding solutions.

Our Mission — to alleviate pain, restore health, and extend life — unites a global team of 95,000+ passionate people.

We are engineers at heart— putting ambitious ideas to work to generate real solutions for real people. From the R&D lab, to the factory floor, to the conference room, every one of us experiments, creates, builds, improves and solves. We have the talent, diverse perspectives, and guts to engineer the extraordinary.

Learn more about our business, mission, and our commitment to diversity here

description:
About the job

Senior Social Media Specialist

Social Media, Reputation Management & Paid Media

📍 Guadalajara, Jalisco, Mexico

📍Tijuana, Baja California, Mexico

🏢 On-Site

🕒 Full-Time

Help Build the Voice Behind the Mission

At FLF, we believe that meaningful connections start with authentic communication.

Every social media post, review response, community interaction, and advertising campaign plays a role in helping injured individuals find trusted legal representation when they need it most.

As we continue to grow, we are seeking a talented Senior Social Media Specialist to help strengthen our digital presence, expand our reach, and build meaningful engagement with the communities we serve.

This role combines social media strategy, reputation management, paid advertising, local search optimization, and performance analytics to support the continued growth of our brand.

If you're passionate about digital marketing, community engagement, content strategy, and data-driven decision making, we'd love to hear from you.

Why This Role Matters

As our Senior Social Media Specialist, you will serve as one of the primary stewards of our public-facing brand.

You will oversee social media channels, manage community engagement, optimize local business profiles, and execute advertising campaigns that help connect potential clients with the legal support they need.

Your work will directly influence brand awareness, lead generation, client trust, and overall marketing performance.

What You'll Do

Social Media Strategy & Content Management


Manage and grow the firm's presence across Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube, and other digital platforms.
Execute content calendars and publishing schedules across multiple channels.
Optimize content using platform-specific best practices and Social SEO strategies.
Support brand awareness, engagement, and lead generation objectives through strategic content distribution.


Reputation Management & Community Engagement


Monitor social media channels, reviews, messages, and community interactions.
Respond professionally and empathetically to comments, direct messages, and online reviews.
Protect and strengthen the firm's online reputation through proactive engagement and issue resolution.
Collaborate with internal teams to ensure timely and accurate responses to client concerns.


Google Business Profile Management


Manage and optimize multiple Google Business Profiles across various locations.
Ensure accurate business information, service descriptions, imagery, and local SEO optimization.
Support local search visibility and reputation-building initiatives.
Monitor profile performance and implement continuous improvements.


Paid Media & Lead Generation


Plan, launch, and optimize paid advertising campaigns across Meta, TikTok, YouTube, and other digital platforms.
Manage audience targeting, retargeting, budget allocation, and campaign optimization.
Conduct A/B testing of creative assets, messaging, and campaign strategies.
Drive lead generation performance while maintaining efficient acquisition costs.


Analytics & Performance Optimization


Track campaign performance, engagement metrics, and lead generation outcomes.
Monitor key marketing metrics, including CPL, CAC, ROAS, and conversion rates.
Develop actionable insights and recommendations based on data analysis.
Utilize AI-powered tools and automation solutions to improve efficiency and scalability.


Compensation & Benefits


Twice a month, salary compensation in USD
Performance-based bonus opportunities
Career growth within a rapidly expanding organization
Ongoing professional development and marketing training
Exposure to advanced digital marketing technologies and AI tools
Collaborative and innovative marketing environment
Modern office located in Guadalajara or Tijuana


The Type of Person Who Thrives Here

Our Most Successful Team Members Are


Creative and data-driven marketers
Exceptional communicators with strong emotional intelligence
Highly organized and execution-focused
Adaptable and curious learners
Strong problem-solvers who take ownership of results
Collaborative team players
Customer-focused professionals who understand the importance of trust and reputation
Motivated by continuous improvement and measurable impact


Why Join FLF?

At FLF, marketing is a strategic growth driver.

You'll have the opportunity to influence how thousands of people interact with our brand, help build meaningful community relationships, and contribute directly to the growth of a rapidly expanding organization.

As our marketing team continues to evolve, you'll gain exposure to advanced digital marketing strategies, emerging technologies, and opportunities for long-term career growth.

About FLF

FLF is a growing Personal Injury Law Firm dedicated to helping injured individuals and their families obtain the justice and compensation they deserve.

Our success is driven by talented professionals across legal, client services, business operations, and marketing who work together to deliver exceptional results. We believe that when great people are empowered to do their best work, extraordinary outcomes follow.

Our Core Values

Loyalty – To our clients, our team, and our mission.

Trust – Building confidence through transparency, consistency, and results.

Respect – For clients, colleagues, and the communities we serve.

Accountability – Taking responsibility for outcomes, both successes and mistakes.

Continuous Growth – Striving to learn, adapt, and improve daily.

Equal Opportunity Employer

FLF is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all team members.

Ready to Apply?

If you're passionate about social media, digital marketing, community engagement, and building brands that make a meaningful impact, we'd love to hear from you.

Please submit your resume along with examples of social media campaigns, paid advertising results, content strategies, or digital marketing projects that demonstrate your expertise and impact.

description:
About the job

About Bedford

We're an email marketing agency for North American eComm brands. Our standard is 10/10 work, and we're scaling fast!




Role Description

You will own everything that brings amazing emails to life: the execution, the timelines, and the quality control. You will be the operational backbone of 10-15 brands at once. 




You will own:

Own the PM pipeline: manage our PM software to deliver emails on time. Chase approvals. Unblock blockers. Keep the weekly rhythm tight. Keep the team accountable.
Run the execution: build and maintain pop ups, flows, and campaigns.
QA everything: you're the last set of eyes before anything goes live. Links, segments, send times, etc. You check it.




You're a fit if:

Are relentlessly organized and fast
Have a sharp eye for mistakes — typos and broken links don't get past you
Follow up before anyone asks
Communicate clearly and directly
Have worked in eCommerce / DTC before
Have worked at an agency or on a fast-moving team
(Bonus) Know Klaviyo or a similar ESP — flows, campaigns, pop-ups, segments, deliverability




More details:

100% remote: but you must overlap with North American business hours
Paid in USD: reliably, every month
Long-term and stable: we want someone to grow with us for years, with real room to take on more as we scale

title: Marketing Specialist
skills: Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.
Content Creation: Craft compelling content including blog posts, newsletters, and social media updates that resonate with our target audience.
SEO Management: Optimize website content for search engines to enhance visibility for relevant keywords related to mental health services for first responders.
Social Media Engagement: Manage social media platforms, creating engaging posts that drive awareness and interaction among followers.
Analytics and Reporting: Analyze marketing performance metrics to assess the effectiveness of campaigns and suggest improvements.
Audience Segmentation: Identify and segment target audiences to tailor messaging specifically for different groups, including agencies and individual first responders.
Networking and Outreach: Develop relationships with potential referral sources, including healthcare providers and first responder agencies, to expand our reach.
Collaboration: Work closely with our team to align marketing efforts with overall practice goals and client needs.
Experience: Minimum 2+ years of experience marketing for American-based companies (Healthcare or Service Industry preferred).
Technical Savvy: Current with modern marketing technologies and social media algorithms; ability to use AI tools to optimize workflow (but not rely on them for core creativity).
Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English skills. While primarily a creative role, you must be comfortable making occasional outbound "warm" contacts to build referral relationships.
Mindset: A "shark" mentality—proactive, aggressive in pursuing visibility, and capable of working without constant micromanagement.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is highly preferred.
Monitoring Tools: Open to using monitoring tools like Hubstaff.

ROLE OVERVIEW




About the Client:

The Client is a growing psychological therapy and testing practice. We specialize in providing high-quality therapy and diagnostic testing (e.g., for first responders and police candidates). We are currently at a pivotal growth phase and are looking for a proactive, tech-savvy marketing professional to help us overcome our current financial bottleneck and scale our team of clinicians.




About the Role:

Our Client is seeking a high-energy Marketing Specialist (Virtual Assistant). This is not a passive task-taking role; they need a strategic thinker who understands the American healthcare landscape and can proactively drive lead generation. You will be responsible for translating the clinical expertise of the Client into a digital presence that attracts the "right fit" clients—specifically those seeking therapy and testing rather than medication management.




Key Responsibilities

Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.
Content Creation: Craft compelling content including blog posts, newsletters, and social media updates that resonate with our target audience.
SEO Management: Optimize website content for search engines to enhance visibility for relevant keywords related to mental health services for first responders.
Social Media Engagement: Manage social media platforms, creating engaging posts that drive awareness and interaction among followers.
Analytics and Reporting: Analyze marketing performance metrics to assess the effectiveness of campaigns and suggest improvements.
Audience Segmentation: Identify and segment target audiences to tailor messaging specifically for different groups, including agencies and individual first responders.
Networking and Outreach: Develop relationships with potential referral sources, including healthcare providers and first responder agencies, to expand our reach.
Collaboration: Work closely with our team to align marketing efforts with overall practice goals and client needs.




Qualifications & Skills

Experience: Minimum 2+ years of experience marketing for American-based companies (Healthcare or Service Industry preferred).
Technical Savvy: Current with modern marketing technologies and social media algorithms; ability to use AI tools to optimize workflow (but not rely on them for core creativity).
Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English skills. While primarily a creative role, you must be comfortable making occasional outbound "warm" contacts to build referral relationships.
Mindset: A "shark" mentality—proactive, aggressive in pursuing visibility, and capable of working without constant micromanagement.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is highly preferred.
Monitoring Tools: Open to using monitoring tools like Hubstaff.

description:
About the job

We are PMG

Named Ad Age Best Places to Work for 11 years in a row and Fast Company Best Workplaces for Innovators since 2022, PMG is a global independent marketing services and technology company that seeks to inspire people and brands that anything is possible. Driven by shared success, PMG leverages business strategy and transformation, creative, media, and insights—as well as its proprietary operating system, Alli—to deliver cutting-edge digital solutions and innovative media strategies.

Our team comprises over 900 employees globally, and our work for ambitious brands like Apple, Nike, Sephora, Experian, Therabody, Best Western Hotels & Resorts, Creed Fragrances, BNY, Intuit, CKE Restaurants, Travelex, and Whole Foods spans 85+ countries.

Professional proficiency in written and spoken English is required, including for resume submission and interviews.

Who You Will Be Working With

At PMG, our Influencer & Branded Content team is at the intersection of culture, storytelling, and strategy, helping brands connect with audiences in authentic, meaningful ways. We craft high-impact influencer campaigns that go beyond traditional advertising - driving engagement, building brand affinity, and delivering measurable results across social, digital, and experiential platforms.

You’ll collaborate with creators, talent agencies, media strategists, content teams, and brand partners to develop and execute influencer marketing programs that seamlessly align storytelling with business objectives. Whether it’s identifying the right talent, negotiating partnerships, overseeing content production, or optimizing campaign performance, your role will be instrumental in shaping how brands foster meaningful connections with their audiences.

Beyond execution, you’ll play a role in exploring new content formats, leveraging emerging platforms, and staying ahead of industry trends to ensure influencer strategies are not only relevant but also innovative and impactful. At PMG, we don’t just follow trends - we create them.

As an Influencer & Branded Content (title level), if you’re passionate about shaping culture through creator-driven storytelling, using data to drive real impact, and redefining what’s possible in influencer marketing, this is the place to make your mark.

What You Will Be Doing


Support the execution of influencer marketing campaigns from start to finish, helping to deliver outstanding, creative work for global brands. 
Serve as a reliable day-to-day contact for clients—managing updates, timelines, and deliverables to ensure smooth communication and collaboration. 
Partner with Account Managers and Directors to develop campaign briefs, creator contracts, and proposals, ensuring clarity and accuracy. 
Contribute to pitches and presentations, helping bring creative ideas to life through data, insights, and storytelling. 
Collaborate across influencer, creative, and strategy teams to deliver cohesive, effective client campaigns. 
Track campaign performance and analyse data to identify key insights and learnings. 
Maintain organized campaign folders, documentation, and reports, ensuring all information is accurate and easy to access. 
Stay current on creator trends, platform updates, and brand partnerships, identifying new opportunities to elevate client work. 
Monitor budgets, timelines, and performance targets to support the achievement of campaign and revenue goals. 
Demonstrate curiosity and initiative—actively seeking opportunities to improve processes and contribute to the team’s success. 


What You Will Bring


1-2+ years of experience in Influencer & Branded Content, with a bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience. 
Understanding of content creation and influencer marketing strategy, supporting campaign execution and brand partnerships. 
Strong attention to detail orientation, ensuring accuracy and consistency in influencer and branded content initiatives. 
Experience in project implementation, helping coordinate and manage influencer collaborations. 
Basic proficiency in presentation skills, effectively communicating campaign results and insights. 
Familiarity with advertising standards, client service, and relationship management, ensuring seamless influencer partnerships. 
Exposure to influencer marketing dashboards and social media platforms, optimizing campaign tracking and performance analysis. 
Experience in networking and influencer management, fostering strong industry relationships for impactful collaborations. 
A commitment to curiosity and adaptability as a Continual Learner, staying at the forefront of media innovation while prioritizing client success with a Customer-Focused mindset that seeks opportunities to deliver meaningful value. 
A dedication to People-Focused leadership, fostering collaboration, innovation, and professional growth by Leading by Example and nurturing strong relationships that empower others to excel. 


What We Offer


Professional Development: Take advantage of our learning and development programs, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement support. 
Generous Time Off: Enjoy generous paid time off and holiday allowances to recharge and spend time with loved ones. 
Parental Leave: We provide paid parental leave to support your family during important life events. 
Retirement & Pension Plans: Plan for your future with competitive retirement or pension programs, including contribution matching. 
Fertility and Family Support: Access fertility benefits for all team members and their spouses. 
Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography. 
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances. 
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being. 
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available. 
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus. 
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities. 
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety. 


Benefits vary by country, location, and geography to reflect local laws, norms, and expectations.

What Sets Us Apart

Being part of PMG means joining a company culture that’s unmatched in digital. We're dedicated to working hard to serve our employees and clients, delivering value, results, and innovation—which often requires true grit and agility. We believe in taking care of ourselves and each other to continuously improve in every way.

In alignment with our core values to be inclusive and always change for the better, PMG is committed to creating a more diverse and inclusive culture, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe that we only change for the better by bringing diverse perspectives to our company. PMG recruits, employs, trains, compensates, and promotes regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

skills: Apoyar la ejecución de iniciativas de Brand Marketing en todas las etapas del funnel (upper, middle y lower), dirigidas a clientes mayoristas y tiendas departamentales clave como Liverpool, Palacio de Hierro, Sears y Coppel. El objetivo es asegurar el posicionamiento correcto de la marca y la alineación entre mensajes, audiencias y canales, impactando en revenue, GM, EBIT, leads, conversión, consideración y ROI.

Crear y colaborar en la adaptación local de campañas globales y en el desarrollo de PDP para cuentas clave, ajustando mensajes y SKUs según prioridades comerciales y necesidades de cada cadena. Se da seguimiento a entregables creativos, shootings y campañas de e-commerce para garantizar visibilidad, awareness y posicionamiento de la marca, contribuyendo a revenue, GM, EBIT, leads, conversión, consideración y ROI.

Gestionar presupuestos y coordinar campañas de temporada, lanzamientos y colaboraciones, alineando planes de marketing (GTM) con briefs creativos y de medios. Implica coordinar agencias y entregables, además de implementar estrategias multicanal (POP, emailing, OOH, BTL) para fortalecer la visibilidad de la marca, siempre dentro de los presupuestos asignados, con impacto en revenue, GM, EBIT, leads, conversión, consideración y ROI.

Colaborar con equipos de Brand Marketing, BE, Wholesale, Retail, Digital, VM y PR para asegurar que las campañas se adapten a canales tradicionales y digitales según las necesidades de la marca y clientes. Esta coordinación garantiza un posicionamiento adecuado y el uso eficiente de presupuestos de inversión (COOP del A&P budget), generando resultados en revenue, GM, EBIT, leads, conversión, consideración y ROI.

description:
About the job

Descripción Empresa

El Puerto de Liverpool es la empresa líder en tiendas departamentales en México. CON MÁS DE 170 AÑOS EN EL MERCADO, nos dedicamos a facilitar la selección de productos y brindarles el mejor servicio a las familias mexicanas, esperando superar las expectativas de calidad, moda, valor y atención dentro de un ambiente agradable.

Contamos con cinco unidades de negocio: Tiendas Liverpool, Tiendas Suburbia, Centros Comerciales Galerías, Boutiques y Negocios Financieros, componiendo una empresa sólida, que ofrece a sus colaboradores estabilidad financiera y el orgullo de pertenecer a una EMPRESA 100% MEXICANA.

NUESTRA MISIÓN ES SERVIR AL CLIENTE EN TODO LUGAR, TODOS LOS DÍAS, TODA LA VIDA, nuestra visión es ser la mejor opción en servicio, surtido y valor. Nuestros valores nos guían hacia el trabajo en equipo, productividad, innovación e integridad.

Misión Del Cargo

Apoyar y Liderar A la Gerencia de Brand Marketing en planeación, ejecución y seguimiento de estrategia de marca para los socios comerciales.

Funciones Del Cargo


 Apoyar la ejecución de iniciativas de Brand Marketing en todas las etapas del funnel (upper, middle y lower), dirigidas a clientes mayoristas y tiendas departamentales clave como Liverpool, Palacio de Hierro, Sears y Coppel. El objetivo es asegurar el posicionamiento correcto de la marca y la alineación entre mensajes, audiencias y canales, impactando en revenue, GM, EBIT, leads, conversión, consideración y ROI.
 Crear y colaborar en la adaptación local de campañas globales y en el desarrollo de PDP para cuentas clave, ajustando mensajes y SKUs según prioridades comerciales y necesidades de cada cadena. Se da seguimiento a entregables creativos, shootings y campañas de e-commerce para garantizar visibilidad, awareness y posicionamiento de la marca, contribuyendo a revenue, GM, EBIT, leads, conversión, consideración y ROI.
 Gestionar presupuestos y coordinar campañas de temporada, lanzamientos y colaboraciones, alineando planes de marketing (GTM) con briefs creativos y de medios. Implica coordinar agencias y entregables, además de implementar estrategias multicanal (POP, emailing, OOH, BTL) para fortalecer la visibilidad de la marca, siempre dentro de los presupuestos asignados, con impacto en revenue, GM, EBIT, leads, conversión, consideración y ROI.
 Colaborar con equipos de Brand Marketing, BE, Wholesale, Retail, Digital, VM y PR para asegurar que las campañas se adapten a canales tradicionales y digitales según las necesidades de la marca y clientes. Esta coordinación garantiza un posicionamiento adecuado y el uso eficiente de presupuestos de inversión (COOP del A&P budget), generando resultados en revenue, GM, EBIT, leads, conversión, consideración y ROI.


Requisitos


Lic. Marketing, Publicidad Comunicación, Administración o afín
2 a 3 años en negociación con clientes, manejo de ppto, entendimiento de un P&L
SAP básico: órdenes de compra, ppto, autorización de órdenes de compra etc.
Inglés B2-C1 INDISPENSABLE (Se aplica prueba de speaking)
Excel Intermedio: Manejo de base de datos, para llevar el ppto, formulas, tablas dinámicas.


Beneficios

Condiciones oferta:


 Seguro médico
 Capacitaciones y cursos
 Seguro médico
 Descuentos en productos
 Vales de despensa
 Horario flexible
 Día de cumpleaños
 Vacaciones extras

description:
About the job

First Marketing hire in Brazil




Disclaimer:

MUST: Full professional-level Brazilian Portuguese
MUST: Professional English & Spanish 
Role Type: Full-time contract role




What's Fitune?

The Shopify for Fitness & Wellness Studios

Fitness & wellness studios struggle with complicated software or manual processes to run their businesses.

We built Fitune, the one-stop-shop to make running their studio less stressful and more profitable.

We are not just software. We are the system for a studio to win, helping studios Attract, Convert, Retain and Operate.




How?

With Fitune, studios get a conversion-optimised website, app and admin software including:

📱 Booking system
💻 Website & App
🎟️ In-person classes, events, courses, 1-on-1, and more
🤑 Automated payments & subscriptions
🐣 Client & business management
🙌 + much more




Where are we at?

Fitune launched in 2021 and now serves hundreds of fitness & wellness studios across Mexico, processing hundreds of thousands of bookings a month. Our ~30-person team is on a mission to make running and growing a studio easy.

We’re on a mission to become the biggest platform in Latam. We won Mexico, we’re now entering Brazil.







The Role

👀 What we're looking for




We are looking for a hungry, content-obsessed marketer to own all marketing content for Brazil and localize our proven Mexican growth engine into the Brazilian market.

We have a marketing machine that works in Mexico, your job is to bring that engine to Brazil and make it sing in native Brazilian Portuguese.

To start, you will translate and localize our Mexican assets. As your understanding of the Brazilian market deepens, more and more should be created locally and from scratch, tuned to local business psychology, culture and how Brazilian studios actually buy.

This is not a dashboard role. Your motto: "Ideas are cheap, execution is everything."




What you'll do

1. Localization & content duplication

Take our high-performing Spanish marketing and growth assets (product marketing, sales collateral, landing pages, website, email) and duplicate, translate and re-architect them into native Brazilian Portuguese
Evolve from translation to locally created content as your read on the market grows
Own brand voice and tone in Brazilian Portuguese (persuasive, creative, friendly)

2. SEO content & keyword research

Implement our SEO playbook for Brazil: local keyword research, on-page optimization, and SEO best practices in your writing
SEO is a key driver of our growth in Mexico. We expect it to be the same in Brazil, and you are central to that

3. High-converting copy (website, landing pages, ads)

Write and optimize website pages, landing pages and ad copy that convert, with a strict focus on readability and Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO)
Build lead magnets and launch assets that attract and convert

4. Product marketing

Communicate the product and its benefits to Brazilian studios in collaboration with product and marketing
Support product launches with positioning, copy and assets, framed around the Studio Success Model

5. Social media, email & lifecycle

Manage Brazil social media and produce the content calendar
Write marketing email and lifecycle copy to move users from awareness to conversion and to retain them

6. Video

Write scripts for videos and record product videos for the Brazilian market

7. HubSpot campaign & CMS management

Build, manage and launch localized growth assets in HubSpot: landing pages, forms, property segmentation, and automated multi-channel lead-nurturing workflows

8. Performance & optimization

Track content and SEO performance, report progress, and use insights to improve impact




Desired experience & qualifications

Bold Brazil market localization: a strong track record of launching or scaling digital products in Brazil, with deep expertise in adapting messaging to local business psychology, cultural nuances, and specific B2B and B2C semantics.
HubSpot campaign & CMS management: hands-on experience building, managing and launching localized growth assets within HubSpot (or similar tools), including localized landing pages, forms, property segmentation, and automated multi-channel lead-nurturing workflows.
International SEO & content optimization: experience implementing localized SEO strategies for the Brazilian market. Proven ability to conduct local keyword research, optimize on-page copy, and ensure high visibility in local search engines.
Growth-driven content production: experience producing high-conversion content across the full funnel, including ad creatives, educational material and onboarding assets, with a strict focus on readability, visual alignment and CRO.
Visual & textual content discipline: direct experience owning the final output of content production pipelines. A sharp eye for layout formatting, margin hygiene, typographic hierarchy and visual consistency when translating content across platforms.
Hands-on startup execution: experience as a high-output, autonomous contributor in fast-paced startup environments, executing day-to-day with a proactive ownership mindset over the final result.




Who are you?

Must

You love writing and obsess over copy that converts
Strong grasp of digital marketing channels: SEO, social media, email marketing and content marketing
Strong SEO understanding for copywriting, best practices and keyword research
Experience writing high-converting landing pages, ads and website copy (CRO mindset)
Product marketing chops for SaaS
HubSpot or other CRM experience (email, flows, automations, CMS)
Great with AI tools and getting things done online, strong computer skills
A self-starter and manager of one. You do not need a big team to execute
Flexible and creative. You will try anything to get our ICP to adopt Fitune

Language requirements:

Native Brazilian Portuguese (must)
Professional English (must)
Professional Spanish (must)

Nice to have

Affinity with the fitness industry or experience in it (big plus)
Strong analytical skills and comfort using data to inform content and SEO
Prior experience localizing a product specifically for Brazil

Who is this not a fit for?

You cannot work hard right now
You need a traditional, highly structured environment. As a startup we are still figuring things out and you are excited to figure it out with us







📍 Location

Remote role, based in Brazil




How to apply ✅

Think you'd be a great fit for this position at Fitune?

 Fill out our Job Application Form at https://forms.gle/oKQcKw8yARTdvkct7

Will make recommendations regarding marketing process. Supports the marketing approach within strategy and budget.
Supports the development of strategic marketing plans for existing lines and participates in the interpretation of plans into “big ideas” to extend the company’s brand vision.

Beginning to act as a marketing advocate, communications consultant, and/or liaison for business support units and/or operational units within the organization. Supports communications with leaders in those units on specific communications issues and strategic growth initiatives and coordinates appropriate marketing resources to achieve their functional goals.

Recommends measurements for return on investment (ROI) for marketing projects within the specific goals of building and delivering campaigns, such as internal and external communications, product launches, new business units, acquisitions, and RFP/RFI proposals within planned scope, schedule, cost and quality, and trade shows/events. Tracks costs against measurements.
Bachelor's Degree

Skills / Knowledge - Having wide-ranging experience, uses professional concepts and company objectives to resolve moderately complex issues in creative and effective ways. Having ownership of a sub-function, account or matrix management responsibilities, applies knowledge to meet goals, maintain relationships, propose opportunities to expand the business, and lead matrix teams. Some barriers to entry exist at this level (e.g., dept./peer review).

Job Complexity - Works on complex issues where analysis of situations or data requires an in-depth evaluation of variable factors. Exercises judgment in selecting methods, techniques and evaluation criteria for obtaining results. Networks with key contacts outside own area of expertise. Builds on/Maintains external relationships of assigned accounts.
Supervision - Determines methods and procedures on new assignments and may coordinate activities of other personnel (Team Lead).

Communication skills - Including communication with internal or external audiences

description:
About the job

Job Title: Platform Engineer (Remote)

Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Flexible workload depending on project availability

title: GenBI Engineer​
skills: Design, build, and maintain enterprise grade data models (facts, dimensions, and semantic layers) optimized for BI and GenAI consumption.
Develop and manage certified data assets in Databricks and/or Microsoft Fabric OneLake, aligned to the Common Data Model (CDM).
Implement metadata enrichment and governance using Databricks Unity Catalog, including business metadata, data classifications, ownership, lineage, and certification.
Build GenBI applications using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents, enabling natural language analytics, conversational BI, and insight generation.
Hands on building SQL expressions within Databricks Genie to improve query processing, reduce latency.
Creating benchmarks and instructions within Databricks Genie/ MSFT CoPilot to improve grounding and accuracy of Conversational AI models.
Partnering with Cybersecurity to create right AD groups, access management and AD group mapping to Genies.
Partner with data engineers to curate gold layer datasets (Delta tables / Lakehouse models) that are analytics and AI ready.
Develop semantic definitions, KPIs, and metrics to ensure consistent enterprise reporting and AI interpretations.
Enable self service analytics by publishing governed datasets and models for Power BI, Databricks SQL, and Copilot experiences.
Apply data quality, validation, and observability checks to ensure accuracy, freshness, and reliability of certified datasets.
Support responsible AI and data governance practices, including PII classification, access controls, and auditability.
Document data products, models, and GenBI use cases, including business definitions and success metrics.

description:
About the job

GenBI Engineer (Generative Business Intelligence Engineer)

Location: CDMX / Remote

Kohler Opportunity

Role Summary:

Join Kohler’s AI Innovation team – Enterprise Architecture Office team to design and deliver Generative Business Intelligence (GenBI) / Conversational AI solutions that enable trusted, self service analytics and AI assisted insights. As a GenBI Engineer I, you will focus on enterprise data modeling, certified data assets, and metadata enrichment, while building conversational and GenAI powered BI experiences using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents.

You will work extensively with Databricks, Unity Catalog, and/or Microsoft Fabric - One Lake, contributing to standardized semantic models aligned with the Common Data Model (CDM). The role emphasizes data trust, governance, discoverability, and AI ready datasets that power dashboards, copilots, and embedded analytics across the enterprise.

Key Responsibilities:


 Design, build, and maintain enterprise grade data models (facts, dimensions, and semantic layers) optimized for BI and GenAI consumption.
 Develop and manage certified data assets in Databricks and/or Microsoft Fabric OneLake, aligned to the Common Data Model (CDM).
 Implement metadata enrichment and governance using Databricks Unity Catalog, including business metadata, data classifications, ownership, lineage, and certification.
 Build GenBI applications using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents, enabling natural language analytics, conversational BI, and insight generation.
 Hands on building SQL expressions within Databricks Genie to improve query processing, reduce latency.
 Creating benchmarks and instructions within Databricks Genie/ MSFT CoPilot to improve grounding and accuracy of Conversational AI models.
 Partnering with Cybersecurity to create right AD groups, access management and AD group mapping to Genies.
 Partner with data engineers to curate gold layer datasets (Delta tables / Lakehouse models) that are analytics and AI ready.
 Develop semantic definitions, KPIs, and metrics to ensure consistent enterprise reporting and AI interpretations.
 Enable self service analytics by publishing governed datasets and models for Power BI, Databricks SQL, and Copilot experiences.
 Apply data quality, validation, and observability checks to ensure accuracy, freshness, and reliability of certified datasets.
 Support responsible AI and data governance practices, including PII classification, access controls, and auditability.
 Document data products, models, and GenBI use cases, including business definitions and success metrics.



Qualifications:


 4–6 years of experience in data engineering, BI engineering, analytics engineering, or strong academic/portfolio projects.
 Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python for data transformation and validation.
 Experience or exposure to data modeling concepts (star schema, snowflake, semantic models, metrics layers).
 Familiarity with Databricks (Delta Lake, Databricks SQL) and/or Microsoft Fabric (One Lake, Warehousing) and proven track record of building GenBI application/Conversational analytics leveraging Databricks Genie.
 Understanding of metadata, data governance, and cataloging concepts.
 Experience with Git based version control and CI/CD for data pipelines or analytics artifacts.
 Strong analytical thinking, problem solving, and ability to collaborate with business and technical stakeholders.



Preferred Experience:


 Hands on experience with Databricks Unity Catalog for data governance, lineage, and certification.
 Experience building and consuming Common Data Model (CDM) aligned datasets.
 Exposure to Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot, or conversational BI models and tools.
 Knowledge of BI tools such as Power BI or Databricks SQL dashboards.
 Familiarity with enterprise domains such as Finance, Procurement, HR, Supply Chain, or Manufacturing analytics.
 Understanding of data quality frameworks, KPI definitions, and metric governance.
 Relevant certifications (e.g., Databricks Data Engineer, Fabric Analytics Engineer, Power BI).



Success Metrics:


 Delivery of certified, well documented data assets adopted by BI and GenAI use cases.
 Demonstrated improvements in data trust, discoverability, and self service analytics adoption.
 Successful enablement of GenBI experiences with accurate, explainable, and governed results.
 Adherence to enterprise architecture, data governance, security, and responsible AI standards.



 Why Choose Kohler? 

We empower each associate to #BecomeMoreAtKohler with a competitive total rewards package to support your health and wellbeing, access to career growth and development opportunities, a diverse and inclusive workplace, and a strong culture of innovation. With more than 30,000 bold leaders across the globe, we’re driving meaningful change in our mission to help people live gracious, healthy, and sustainable lives.

If, as an individual with a disability, you need reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact kohlerjobs@kohler.com . title: Junior Media Buyer for a US Performance Marketing Agency (Remote)
skills: Building, launching, and managing Meta campaigns (CBO/ABO structures, audiences, placements, creative testing).
Supporting and growing into independent buying on AppLovin / Axon.
Monitoring daily performance and flagging scaling opportunities, underperformers, and anomalies.
Running structured creative and audience tests, and documenting the results.
Pulling, organizing, and interpreting performance data to inform decisions, and producing clear weekly client reports.
Analyzing creative performance using soft metrics (CTR, CPM, CPC), benchmarking against account averages, and clearly communicating those reads to strategists and clients.
Helping maintain clean account hygiene, naming conventions, and tracking.
0-2 years of hands-on paid media experience (internships, freelance, or running your own / your brand's ads all count).
Clear written communication in English.
Genuine fluency with Meta Ads Manager — you've built campaigns, not just read about them.
Strong numerical sense; comfortable living in spreadsheets and dashboards.
A direct-response mindset: you care about CPA, ROAS, and contribution margin, not vanity metrics.
Familiarity with DTC e-commerce, Shopify, and the fashion/beauty/lifestyle space.
Working knowledge of tracking and attribution tools (pixels, CAPI, ClickFlare, GA4, etc.).
Comfortable staying responsive when accounts demand it, including the occasional evening or weekend during launches or scaling pushes.
Availability during key EST windows to analyze performance and take action (scale, kill, pull back, etc.).
Experience with AdManage (strongly preferred).
Hands-on time with AppLovin / Axon or other programmatic / native platforms.
Basic understanding of creative.
Competitive base salary.
Fully remote, full-time contractor role.
Learn direct-response media buying directly from senior operators managing significant spend.
Real ownership early—you won't be stuck pulling reports for a year.
Work across a diverse roster of scaling, fast-growing DTC brands in fashion, beauty, and lifestyle.
A fast-paced team environment that rewards results and fosters growth into a senior media buyer role.
description:

description:
About the job

Creator Growth Specialist

Salary: $2,000 USD per month

Type: Full-time

Location: 100% Remote

Role Overview

We are hiring a Creator Growth Specialist to support a portfolio of content creators across audience growth, content strategy, creator operations, and monetization initiatives.

This is not a traditional influencer marketing role. We are looking for someone with hands-on experience working directly with creators to help them grow their audiences, strengthen their personal brands, optimize content performance, and expand revenue opportunities across today's creator economy.

The ideal candidate understands how creators build sustainable businesses through content strategy, audience engagement, platform optimization, and multiple monetization channels.

Key Responsibilities

Manage a portfolio of content creators as their primary point of contact.
Develop and execute audience growth strategies across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and emerging creator platforms.
Advise creators on content planning, performance optimization, audience engagement, and platform best practices.
Create and manage content calendars to support consistent publishing.

Track campaigns, partnerships, affiliate programs, and creator performance within internal systems.
Maintain accurate records in CRM platforms, Monday.com, and other operational tools.
Coordinate communication between creators, internal teams, and brand partners.

Requirements

Resume in English.
Native or near-native English proficiency (spoken and written).
3+ years of experience in creator talent management, creator partnerships, creator success, creator operations, or the creator economy.
Proven experience working directly with content creators, influencers, personal brands, or creator rosters.
Demonstrated success helping creators grow audiences, improve content performance, and increase monetization.
Strong understanding of creator business models and the creator economy.
Deep knowledge of TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and emerging creator platforms.

Strong organizational, communication, and relationship management skills.
Comfortable managing multiple creators and priorities simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.

Descripción Empresa

El Puerto de Liverpool es la empresa líder en tiendas departamentales en México. CON MÁS DE 170 AÑOS EN EL MERCADO, nos dedicamos a facilitar la selección de productos y brindarles el mejor servicio a las familias mexicanas, esperando superar las expectativas de calidad, moda, valor y atención dentro de un ambiente agradable.

Contamos con cinco unidades de negocio: Tiendas Liverpool, Tiendas Suburbia, Centros Comerciales Galerías, Boutiques y Negocios Financieros, componiendo una empresa sólida, que ofrece a sus colaboradores estabilidad financiera y el orgullo de pertenecer a una EMPRESA 100% MEXICANA.

NUESTRA MISIÓN ES SERVIR AL CLIENTE EN TODO LUGAR, TODOS LOS DÍAS, TODA LA VIDA, nuestra visión es ser la mejor opción en servicio, surtido y valor. Nuestros valores nos guían hacia el trabajo en equipo, productividad, innovación e integridad.

Experience or exposure to data modeling concepts (star schema, snowflake, semantic models, metrics layers).

Familiarity with Databricks (Delta Lake, Databricks SQL) and/or Microsoft Fabric (One Lake, Warehousing) and proven track record of building GenBI application/Conversational analytics leveraging Databricks Genie.
Understanding of metadata, data governance, and cataloging concepts.
Experience with Git based version control and CI/CD for data pipelines or analytics artifacts.
Strong analytical thinking, problem solving, and ability to collaborate with business and technical stakeholders.
Hands on experience with Databricks Unity Catalog for data governance, lineage, and certification.
Experience building and consuming Common Data Model (CDM) aligned datasets.
Exposure to Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot, or conversational BI models and tools.

Familiarity with enterprise domains such as Finance, Procurement, HR, Supply Chain, or Manufacturing analytics.
Understanding of data quality frameworks, KPI definitions, and metric governance.
Relevant certifications (e.g., Databricks Data Engineer, Fabric Analytics Engineer, Power BI).
Delivery of certified, well documented data assets adopted by BI and GenAI use cases.

Adherence to enterprise architecture, data governance, security, and responsible AI standards.
description:
About the job

GenBI Engineer (Generative Business Intelligence Engineer)

Location: CDMX / Remote

Kohler Opportunity

Role Summary:

Join Kohler’s AI Innovation team – Enterprise Architecture Office team to design and deliver Generative Business Intelligence (GenBI) / Conversational AI solutions that enable trusted, self service analytics and AI assisted insights. As a GenBI Engineer I, you will focus on enterprise data modeling, certified data assets, and metadata enrichment, while building conversational and GenAI powered BI experiences using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents.

You will work extensively with Databricks, Unity Catalog, and/or Microsoft Fabric - One Lake, contributing to standardized semantic models aligned with the Common Data Model (CDM). The role emphasizes data trust, governance, discoverability, and AI ready datasets that power dashboards, copilots, and embedded analytics across the enterprise.

Key Responsibilities:


 Design, build, and maintain enterprise grade data models (facts, dimensions, and semantic layers) optimized for BI and GenAI consumption.
 Develop and manage certified data assets in Databricks and/or Microsoft Fabric OneLake, aligned to the Common Data Model (CDM).
 Implement metadata enrichment and governance using Databricks Unity Catalog, including business metadata, data classifications, ownership, lineage, and certification.
 Build GenBI applications using Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot and/or Fabric Data Agents, enabling natural language analytics, conversational BI, and insight generation.
 Hands on building SQL expressions within Databricks Genie to improve query processing, reduce latency.
 Creating benchmarks and instructions within Databricks Genie/ MSFT CoPilot to improve grounding and accuracy of Conversational AI models.
 Partnering with Cybersecurity to create right AD groups, access management and AD group mapping to Genies.
 Partner with data engineers to curate gold layer datasets (Delta tables / Lakehouse models) that are analytics and AI ready.
 Develop semantic definitions, KPIs, and metrics to ensure consistent enterprise reporting and AI interpretations.
 Enable self service analytics by publishing governed datasets and models for Power BI, Databricks SQL, and Copilot experiences.
 Apply data quality, validation, and observability checks to ensure accuracy, freshness, and reliability of certified datasets.
 Support responsible AI and data governance practices, including PII classification, access controls, and auditability.
 Document data products, models, and GenBI use cases, including business definitions and success metrics.



Qualifications:


 4–6 years of experience in data engineering, BI engineering, analytics engineering, or strong academic/portfolio projects.
 Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python for data transformation and validation.
 Experience or exposure to data modeling concepts (star schema, snowflake, semantic models, metrics layers).
 Familiarity with Databricks (Delta Lake, Databricks SQL) and/or Microsoft Fabric (One Lake, Warehousing) and proven track record of building GenBI application/Conversational analytics leveraging Databricks Genie.
 Understanding of metadata, data governance, and cataloging concepts.
 Experience with Git based version control and CI/CD for data pipelines or analytics artifacts.
 Strong analytical thinking, problem solving, and ability to collaborate with business and technical stakeholders.



Preferred Experience:


 Hands on experience with Databricks Unity Catalog for data governance, lineage, and certification.
 Experience building and consuming Common Data Model (CDM) aligned datasets.
 Exposure to Databricks Genie, Microsoft Copilot, or conversational BI models and tools.
 Knowledge of BI tools such as Power BI or Databricks SQL dashboards.
 Familiarity with enterprise domains such as Finance, Procurement, HR, Supply Chain, or Manufacturing analytics.
 Understanding of data quality frameworks, KPI definitions, and metric governance.
 Relevant certifications (e.g., Databricks Data Engineer, Fabric Analytics Engineer, Power BI).



Success Metrics:


 Delivery of certified, well documented data assets adopted by BI and GenAI use cases.
 Demonstrated improvements in data trust, discoverability, and self service analytics adoption.
 Successful enablement of GenBI experiences with accurate, explainable, and governed results.
 Adherence to enterprise architecture, data governance, security, and responsible AI standards.



 Why Choose Kohler? 

We empower each associate to #BecomeMoreAtKohler with a competitive total rewards package to support your health and wellbeing, access to career growth and development opportunities, a diverse and inclusive workplace, and a strong culture of innovation. With more than 30,000 bold leaders across the globe, we’re driving meaningful change in our mission to help people live gracious, healthy, and sustainable lives.

About Us

If, as an individual with a disability, you need reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact kohlerjobs@kohler.com . description:
About the job

Estoy ayudando a CXC LATAM - Customer Xperience Crew a encontrar al mejor candidato para unirse a su equipo tiempo completo para el rol de CX Specialist.




Impulsarás la experiencia del cliente en LATAM, transformando insights en estrategias de impacto con tecnología.




Compensación:

USD 1.2K - 1.4K/mes




Ubicación:

Remoto: México




Misión de CXC LATAM - Customer Xperience Crew:

"Creemos firmemente que el éxito de cualquier idea, producto o servicio está en el desarrollo de una cultura centrada en el cliente que sea apoyada por tecnologías, empleados satisfechos y procesos que respondan a sus necesidades."




¿Qué te hace un candidato ideal?

Eres competente en Zendesk, Research, Qualtrics XM Expert, OKR (Objetivos y resultados clave), Liderazgo estratégico.
Tienes +2 años de experiencia en Customer experience (CX).
Inglés - Completamente fluido
Español - Completamente fluido




Responsabilidades y más:

Objetivo del Puesto:

- Diseñar, implementar y administrar iniciativas de Customer Experience (CX) para clientes y proyectos internos de la organización, utilizando plataformas como Qualtrics, Zendesk y herramientas complementarias de analítica y automatización.

- Será responsable de traducir la Voz del Cliente (VoC) en insights accionables, acompañar la ejecución de programas CX y asegurar la adopción de mejores prácticas orientadas a mejorar la experiencia del cliente y los resultados de negocio.

- Basado en el perfil original de CX Leader adaptado a un rol de especialista ejecutor.




Responsabilidades Principales:

Diseño y Ejecución de Programas CX:

- Participar en diagnósticos de experiencia del cliente para organizaciones clientes e iniciativas internas.

- Diseñar y documentar customer journeys, journey maps y mapas de procesos relacionados con CX.

- Configurar programas de medición basados en NPS, CSAT, CES y otros indicadores de experiencia.

- Colaborar en la definición de estrategias Voice of Customer (VoC).

- Identificar oportunidades de mejora en los distintos puntos de contacto del cliente.




Implementación y Administración de Plataformas:

- Diseñar, configurar y optimizar encuestas, dashboards y workflows en Qualtrics.

- Configurar programas de experiencia en Zendesk y plataformas complementarias.

- Gestionar el ciclo operativo de proyectos CX desde el diseño hasta la entrega.

- Apoyar procesos de integración con CRM, plataformas de soporte, Contact Center y herramientas de BI.

- Realizar pruebas funcionales, QA y validaciones de soluciones implementadas.




Análisis e Insights:

- Analizar datos cuantitativos y cualitativos provenientes de programas CX.

- Generar reportes ejecutivos y presentaciones para clientes internos y externos.

- Traducir hallazgos en recomendaciones accionables.

- Dar seguimiento a iniciativas de mejora derivadas de resultados de experiencia.

- Participar en procesos de Close The Loop con áreas operativas y de negocio.




Gestión de Proyectos:

- Coordinar tareas, cronogramas y entregables de proyectos CX.

- Dar seguimiento a actividades mediante Monday, Zendesk, SharePoint u otras herramientas corporativas.

- Gestionar documentación funcional y operativa.

- Mantener actualizados planes de trabajo, riesgos, dependencias y estatus de proyecto.

- Participar en reuniones de seguimiento con clientes y stakeholders.




Capacitación y Adopción:

- Facilitar entrenamientos funcionales sobre Qualtrics, Zendesk y metodologías CX.

- Elaborar manuales, guías, procedimientos y materiales de capacitación.

- Apoyar procesos de gestión del cambio y adopción tecnológica.

- Colaborar en programas internos de formación y desarrollo de capacidades CX.




Innovación y Mejora Continua:

- Proponer mejoras metodológicas para servicios y procesos CX.

- Participar en la creación de activos reutilizables, templates, playbooks y mejores prácticas.

- Colaborar en iniciativas de innovación relacionadas con IA, automatización y experiencia del cliente.

- Apoyar proyectos internos de transformación organizacional centrados en el cliente.




Requisitos del Puesto:

Formación Académica:

- Licenciatura en Mercadotecnia, Administración, Ingeniería Industrial, Psicología, Comunicación, Sistemas o carreras afines.

- Deseable especialización o certificaciones en Customer Experience, Service Design, Customer Success, Business Intelligence o Gestión de Proyectos.




Experiencia Profesional:

- 2 a 4 años de experiencia en Customer Experience, Customer Success, Customer Insights, Service Design, Consultoría o áreas relacionadas.

- Experiencia comprobable en implementación o administración de proyectos con Qualtrics.

- Experiencia en gestión de proyectos tecnológicos o de transformación.

- Deseable experiencia trabajando con plataformas SaaS.

- Deseable experiencia en consultoría o atención a clientes corporativos.




Conocimientos Técnicos:

- Manejo de Qualtrics XM Platform.

- Conocimiento de métricas CX (NPS, CSAT, CES, VoC).

- Diseño de customer journeys y service blueprints.

- Análisis de datos cuantitativos y cualitativos.

- Conocimientos de Zendesk (Support, Guide, Messaging, AI Agents).

- Herramientas de visualización como Power BI, Tableau o Looker Studio.

- Gestión de proyectos con Monday, Jira, Asana o similares.

- Conocimientos básicos de integraciones API y automatización (deseable).

- Inglés intermedio-avanzado (B2 o superior).




Competencias Clave:

- Pensamiento analítico.

- Orientación al cliente.

- Comunicación efectiva.

- Organización y gestión del tiempo.

- Resolución de problemas.

- Gestión de múltiples proyectos simultáneos.

- Capacidad consultiva.

- Proactividad.

- Adaptabilidad.

- Trabajo colaborativo.

- Atención al detalle.

- Curiosidad por innovación y nuevas tecnologías.




Beneficios del trabajo:

Trabajo en equipo
Comodidad para trabajar en casa
Capacidad para iterar

Trabajos remoto y hay flexibilidad
